item.title: N.J. governor requests access to Delaney Hall ICE facility amid protests
item.description: New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill is asking ICE to let her inside the Delaney Hall detention center in Newark.

item.title: Iran says obstacles remain, but acknowledges deal taking shape with U.S.
item.description: As the Trump administration signals hope for a possible deal, Iran says obstacles linger, and "frequent changes" in U.S. positions aren't helping.

item.title: California firefighters say threat of explosion in Orange County chemical tank has been "eliminated"
item.description: More than 50,000 people in California remain evacuated due to an Orange County chemical leak that prompted Gov. Newsom to request that President Trump issue a Federal Emergency Declaration.

item.title: Explosion threat "eliminated" for California chemical leak, officials say
item.description: California crews conducted an overnight operation at the Orange County site of a chemical leak in Garden Grove and said Monday that the threat of an explosion had been "eliminated" and was "off the table."

item.title: Pope Leo calls for "disarming" of AI in technology-focused encyclical
item.description: In his "Magnifica Humanitas" encyclical, Pope Leo warns that as civilization grapples with the power of AI, the main challenge is remaining "profoundly human."

item.title: 1,500 strangers show up to bury World War II veteran
item.description: John Bernard Arnold III, a World War II U.S. Navy veteran who died on May 6 at 98, had no known living relatives to celebrate his life and service. A veterans service officer in Hanson, Massachusetts, put out a call for support, and 1,500 people showed up to bury a man they'd never met. CBS News contributor David Begnaud reports.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help you kick off your summer with ease. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: The Dish Recipe: Stepping up your Memorial Day burger game
item.description: "Tiny Spoon Chef" Janice Carte shares two recipes that can help elevate your Memorial Day burgers.

item.title: Money-saving tips for car buying
item.description: CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger shares tips for navigating a challenging car-buying market.

item.title: How Americans are handling a gas price spike
item.description: Gas prices are the highest since 2022, shifting how some Americans are choosing to spend their money. Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: Pope Leo warns about dangers of AI
item.description: In Pope Leo XIV's first encyclical, or papal declaration, the pontiff warned about the dangers of artificial intelligence. Chris Livesay reports.

item.title: Land where notorious cartel leader was killed is being auctioned
item.description: The Mexican government announced the auction of a plot of land within the country club where drug kingpin "El Mencho" died during a clash with the army in February.

item.title: Tributes pour out for Kyle Busch after sudden death as family makes appearance at Coca-Cola 600
item.description: Tributes have poured out since Kyle Busch's sudden death last Thursday. His wife and children were honored on Sunday at the Coca-Cola 600, which Busch was planning to compete in. Michael George reports.

item.title: What to know about a leaking California chemical tank that may explode
item.description: The tank at GKN Aerospace is estimated to contain 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate, a volatile chemical used to produce plastics.

item.title: AG requests to resume ballroom construction, citing latest shooting
item.description: Attorney General Todd Blanche said the ballroom is "being constructed for the physical safety and security of all Presidents, their families, staff, Foreign Dignitaries, and guests."

item.title: White House shooting suspect had history of run-ins with Secret Service, court documents show
item.description: The suspect who allegedly opened fire at a Secret Service checkpoint outside the White House on Saturday had previous run-ins with law enforcement in the same area, court records show. Nicole Sganga has more.

item.title: Breaking down the sticking points between U.S. and Iran as negotiators work toward peace deal
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said an agreement between the U.S. and Iran that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz could be announced as early as Monday. Sam Vinograd breaks down some of the sticking points.

item.title: Questions arise as Trump teases possible Iran deal
item.description: President Trump said over the weekend on social media that a peace deal with Iran was "largely negotiated," which Iran acknowledged, but pushed back on. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Memorial Day travel hits snags as gas prices reach highest level since 2022
item.description: Gas prices in the U.S. are at their highest levels since 2022, prompting some Americans to rethink their Memorial Day travel. Olivia Rinaldi has the latest.

item.title: Chemical leak in California's Orange County forces 50,000 people to evacuate
item.description: A leaking chemical tank in Cypress, California, is at risk of exploding, officials say, and at least 50,000 people have been forced to evacuate. Lana Zak has the latest.

item.title: DOJ says it scrubbed news releases about Jan. 6 criminal cases from its website
item.description: The Justice Department acknowledges it has removed from its website news releases about criminal cases related to the Jan. 6, 2021 riot, calling the information "partisan propaganda."

item.title: Experts from Thai rescue join efforts to save 7 trapped in Laos cave
item.description: Seven Laotian villagers entered a cave five days ago searching for gold and were trapped inside after heavy rain triggered flash flooding.

item.title: Ebola death toll climbs in Congo as new infections confirmed in Uganda
item.description: The WHO chief says there have been 220 suspected deaths in the ongoing Ebola outbreak as Ugandan health authorities report two new Ebola infections.

item.title: Suspects wanted by FBI for robbing athletes' homes arrested in Chile
item.description: The thieves stole jewelry, high-end watches and souvenirs such as sports jerseys, among other belongings, police said.

item.title: Vietnam vets say opposing Trump's arch is about being "loyal to the country"
item.description: "I think it's just disrespectful to those that I served with who didn't come back," a veteran suing to stop construction of the arch told CBS News.

item.title: Husband convicted of hiring hitman to kill NYC art dealer in Brazil
item.description: Daniel Sikkema faces a mandatory sentence of life in prison. Brent Sikkema was stabbed to death in his Rio de Janeiro townhouse in 2024.

item.title: What's open and closed for Memorial Day 2026?
item.description: Most retail stores will be open for business on Memorial Day, while post offices will be closed. Here's what to know.

item.title: California Gov. Newsom declares state of emergency for Orange County chemical leak as DA launches probe into its cause
item.description: Southern California officials are desperately searching for a safe resolution to a leaking toxic chemical tank at an aerospace facility.

item.title: CBS News Radio signs off after nearly 100 years: "An American institution"
item.description: After nearly a century on the air, CBS News Radio signed off, with the final reports airing on Friday, May 22.

item.title: Ebola "spreading rapidly" in Congo with national risk "very high"
item.description: The head of the World Health Organization says Ebola has killed at least 7 people in Congo, but the U.N. agency says it knows the epidemic "is much larger."
